title: GitHub Review by gndrmnn
summary: The reviewer wants lines 3287-3290 removed from `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go` because testing the value of a self-declared constant (`KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey`) provides no value.

code_comments.body: Remove lines 3287-3290. We have no interest in testing the value of constants we declare ourselves. There is no good reason to test if `KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey` has the value we assign to it.
code_comments.author: gndrmnn
code_comments.resolution: Deleted the `g.Expect(KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey).To(Equal("spec.secretRefs.name"), ...)` block (original lines 3287-3290) plus the preceding 'const value is a stable contract surface' comment; updated the test's doc comment to describe the remaining, narrower intent (verifying the constant is the source of the registration key, not its literal value).

resolution_summary: Removed the assertion that tested the literal value of the `KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey` constant in `TestKeystoneSecretIndexerKey_IsDeclaredAsConst` (operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go), along with its leading explanatory comment. The test's docstring was rewritten to reflect the narrower remaining scope: verifying that `registerSecretNameIndex` sources its key from the exported constant and registers against the Keystone type, rather than asserting the constant's literal value.
